meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing for No-Code Beginners
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing strategies to overcome technical gaps. This beginner-level case study shows how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance business.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of SaaS knowledge, implement a tiered, value-based pricing model that prioritizes recurring revenue over hourly billing. Use Antigravity to generate your infrastructure and Claude Code to iterate on feature logic. This combination allows you to ship professional-grade pricing pages that convert visitors into high-paying enterprise clients.
content.intro:
The client is staring at a blank screen, demanding a high-converting SaaS platform, yet your background is in simple web design. The fear of underpricing your value or crashing a complex billing integration is a weight every freelancer knows all too well. You don't need a computer science degree; you need a system that translates technical complexity into profitable, scalable architecture.

Today, we bridge that gap. We are deploying a robust pricing strategy using the raw power of AI-assisted development. By combining the architectural speed of Antigravity with the surgical precision of Claude Code, you will transform from a 'website builder' into a high-ticket SaaS strategist, delivering products that practically pay for themselves.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, define three tiers: Starter, Pro, and Enterprise. Focus on outcomes, not just feature lists.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing architecture schema that includes three tiers: Starter ($29/mo), Pro ($99/mo), and Enterprise (Custom). Include logic for annual discounts and feature-gating parameters.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a data structure for these three pricing tiers in my current project backend.'

steps.title: Initialize the Billing Infrastructure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the Stripe/LemonSqueezy integration logic, ensuring your payment flow is secure and compliant.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a secure Stripe checkout integration module. Include webhooks for subscription updates and error handling for failed transactions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npm install stripe && npx create-stripe-webhook-handler'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Connect your pricing tiers to your application features so users can only access what they pay for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks the user's subscription tier before granting access to specific API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file auth_middleware.ts --instruction 'Apply the subscription tier check to the API routes.'

steps.title: Build the High-Conversion UI
steps.description: Create a pricing component that highlights the 'Pro' tier, utilizing psychological triggers like anchor pricing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive React pricing component with a toggle for monthly/yearly billing. Highlight the Pro tier using a 'Most Popular' badge.

steps.title: Testing and Deployment
steps.description: Simulate subscription flows to ensure your pricing logic holds up under pressure before handing it to the client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the pricing logic, covering edge cases like subscription downgrades and payment failures.

pro_tips: Always anchor the Enterprise price as a 'Contact Us' button to capture high-intent leads manually.
Use 'feature decay' in your Starter tier to gently force an upgrade path as the user scales.
Never hardcode prices; store them in a database or external config so you can A/B test pricing without a redeploy.
Offer a 'Founders Edition' pricing tier for the first 10 users to build social proof and immediate cash flow.
Implement an automated email drip campaign triggered specifically by subscription failure webhooks to recover lost revenue.
content.next_steps: After deploying your pricing model, set up an analytics dashboard to track conversion rates per tier and iterate on your copy based on user drop-off points.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to command premium rates and deliver enterprise-grade SaaS platforms. Stop trading time for money and start building systems that scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
